question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a mix of eq and non-eq spender.id filters (e.g., spender.id=eq:100&spender.id=gt:200) to cause the position argument passed to updateConditionsAndParamsWithInValues (conditions.length + 1) to diverge from the actual params array index, resulting in a misaligned SQL parameterized query that returns wrong allowance rows or silently skips the amount > 0 guard?
